title: fix: sub-batch embed requests — Pollinations caps input at 32 items
author: Itachi-1824
pr_body_excerpt:
Second full backfill (after #12609) got to 475/1852 files, 1384 vectors upserted, then died on Pollinations' 32-item input cap. Fixed that, then reviewed the whole embed path properly before pushing again rather than iterating fix-by-fix in CI:

- **32-item batch cap** — \`embed_batch\` now sub-batches at 32 instead of sending a whole file's chunks in one request.
- **Parallelized the embed loop** — was fully sequential (one file at a time), which is most of why 1852 files took so long. Now runs with concurrency=4 via a thread pool. Kept conservative rather than higher since real embeddings-endpoint concurrency hasn't been load-tested against the actual credential.
- **Fixed a silent chunk/embedding misalignment bug** — \`texts\` was filtered (dropping blank chunks) before embedding, but the result was zipped against the *unfiltered* chunk list. Any blank chunk shifted every embedding after it by one position, so a later chunk's embedding could get stored under an earlier chunk's file_path/line-range metadata. Filtering once and reusing the same list fixes it.
- **429-aware retries** — rate limits now back off using \`Retry-After\` instead of being treated like any other error.
- **No more silent partial success** — a run with any per-file failures now exits non-zero instead of reporting success. Re-running is safe (upserts are idempotent by id), so a red CI check just means "run it again," not "some files are silently missing forever."

Index currently has 1384 partial vectors from the failed run — will clear + re-run \`full\` after this merges.

gist.headline: Vectors, properly pollinated
gist.blurb: The code garden’s embedding run learned to carry seeds in batches of 32, retry when the weather gets busy, and stop pretending a half-planted row is finished.
gist.summary: The code-index embedding script now splits requests into Pollinations’ 32-item limit, processes files with four concurrent workers, preserves chunk-to-embedding metadata alignment, honors 429 Retry-After headers, and fails visibly when any file cannot be indexed.
gist.impact: Future full backfills can reliably index all 1,852 files without oversized embed requests, shifted source metadata after blank chunks, or silently incomplete runs.
gist.visual_concept: A code-garden backfill: Polly organizes glowing vector seeds into neat trays of 32 while four tiny garden conveyors carry file-chunks toward a retro embedding terminal. The scene represents batching, parallel workers, correctly matched metadata labels, and patient Retry-After waiting rather than silently abandoning plants.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush, light blue wings, and a tiny gardener tool belt, sorting glowing code-seeds into four neat trays labeled with small pixel text '32' beside a warm CRT embedding terminal; every seed has a matching little file-path tag, with flowers and code-plants growing nearby. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, dark purple (#110518) outlines, nostalgic but beautiful.
